questions.text: What does Allah swear by in the first two verses?
questions.answer: The Dawn and the ten nights (generally understood as the first ten days of Dhul-Hijjah).

questions.text: What are 'the even and the odd' mentioned in Verse 3?
questions.answer: Scholars say it refers to all of creation (even/pairs) and the Creator (Odd/One), or specific days of Hajj.

questions.text: Who were the people of 'Iram'?
questions.answer: The people of 'Ad, who possessed lofty pillars/cities the likes of which were not created in the lands.

questions.text: What were the people of Thamud famous for?
questions.answer: Carving rocks/homes in the valley.

questions.text: How is Pharaoh described in Verse 10?
questions.answer: As 'the owner of the stakes' (Dhul-Awtad).

questions.text: What did these three nations ('Ad, Thamud, Pharaoh) do in the lands?
questions.answer: They transgressed and increased corruption.

questions.text: What was the result of their corruption?
questions.answer: Allah poured upon them a 'scourge of punishment'.

questions.text: Is your Lord ever unaware of human actions?
questions.answer: No, 'Indeed, your Lord is in observation/ambush (Mirṣād)'.

questions.text: How does the 'man' react when Allah tests him with honor and blessings?
questions.answer: He says: 'My Lord has honored me'.

questions.text: How does the 'man' react when Allah tests him by restricting his provision?
questions.answer: He says: 'My Lord has humiliated me'.

questions.text: What are the four social failures Allah criticizes in Verses 17-20?
questions.answer: Not honoring the orphan, not encouraging the feeding of the poor, consuming inheritance greedily, and loving wealth excessively.

questions.text: What will happen to the earth on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: It will be crushed with a 'crushing, crushing' (Dakkan Dakka).

questions.text: Who will come with the angels rank upon rank?
questions.answer: Your Lord (meaning His command or manifestation).

questions.text: What will be brought forth on that Day?
questions.answer: Hell (Jahannam).

questions.text: What will the human wish for when he sees Hell?
questions.answer: He will say: 'Oh, I wish I had sent forth [some good] for my life'.

questions.text: Can anyone punish like Allah's punishment or bind like His binding on that Day?
questions.answer: No (Verses 25-26).

questions.text: Who is addressed as 'An-Nafs al-Mutma'innah'?
questions.answer: The 'soul at peace' (the righteous soul).

questions.text: What is the command to the soul at peace?
questions.answer: 'Return to your Lord, well-pleased and pleasing [to Him]'.

questions.text: Where is the soul at peace told to enter?
questions.answer: 'Enter among My [righteous] servants and enter My Paradise'.

questions.text: What is the main message of this Surah regarding wealth?
questions.answer: Wealth is a test, not necessarily a sign of Allah's love, and its misuse leads to destruction.
